---
title: "Supplementary Figure 6"
output: html_document
date: '2023-03-15'
---

```{r}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, eval = TRUE, message = FALSE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file())

# Set path
if (Sys.info()["sysname"] == "Darwin"){
  mount_path <- "/Volumes/G_DQBM_BB_Central$/projects/immucan/"
} else if (Sys.info()["sysname"] == "Linux"){
  mount_path <- "/mnt/immucan_volume/"
} else if (Sys.info()["sysname"] == "Windows"){
  mount_path <- "Y:/"
} else {
  stop("Operating system is not supported")
}
```


load libraries
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(reticulate)
library(MOFA2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
```

## load model
```{r}
mount_path <- "/mnt/immucan_volume/processed_data/NSCLC2_cohort/Figures/scores_for_sharing/MOFA"
out_path <- "/mnt/immucan_volume/processed_data/NSCLC2_cohort/Figures/Figure_6_supp/"

model_path <- paste0(mount_path, '/mofa_V6_minor.rds')

if (!dir.exists(out_path)){
  dir.create(out_path)
  dir.create(paste0(out_path, "/extras"))
}
model <- readRDS(model_path)
```

# S6A supp: GSEA per Factor
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(MOFA2)
library(dplyr)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(msigdbr)
library(tibble)
library(readr)

# === Define your MOFA object and output path ===
mofa_model <- model  # replace with your MOFA object
output_dir <- mount_path
dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# === Step 1: Load GO Biological Process gene sets ===
msig_gobp <- msigdbr(species = "Homo sapiens", category = "C5", subcategory = "GO:BP")
term2gene <- msig_gobp[, c("gs_name", "gene_symbol")]
term2name <- msig_gobp[, c("gs_name", "gs_description")]

# === Step 2: Extract RNA weights from MOFA model ===
weights <- get_weights(mofa_model, views = "RNA", as.data.frame = TRUE)

# === Step 3: Loop over factors and run GSEA ===
for (f in unique(weights$factor)) {
  # Filter and rank genes by factor weight
  factor_weights <- weights %>%
    filter(factor == f) %>%
    arrange(desc(value))  # or use abs(value) if needed

  ranked_genes <- factor_weights$value
  names(ranked_genes) <- factor_weights$feature

  # Clean ranking list
  ranked_genes <- ranked_genes[!is.na(ranked_genes)]
  ranked_genes <- ranked_genes[!duplicated(names(ranked_genes))]

  # Run GSEA
  set.seed(123)
  gsea_res <- GSEA(
    geneList = ranked_genes,
    TERM2GENE = term2gene,
    TERM2NAME = term2name,
    pvalueCutoff = 1,
    pAdjustMethod = "BH",
    minGSSize = 10,
    maxGSSize = 500,
    scoreType = "std"
  )

  # Save results
  save(gsea_res, file = file.path(output_dir, paste0("GSEA_GOBP_Factor", f, ".RData")))
  write.table(gsea_res@result,
              file = file.path(output_dir, paste0("GSEA_GOBP_Factor", f, ".txt")),
              sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE)
}
```



## plot heatmap

```{r, fig.height=8, fig.width=8}

library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)
# === Define parameters ===
pathway <- "GOBP"
input_dir <- output_dir  # defined earlier as your GSEA output directory
factors <- unique(weights$factor)
comparison_dir <- file.path(input_dir, "GOBP/comparison")
if (!file.exists(comparison_dir)) dir.create(comparison_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# === Retrieve top 10 enriched pathways per factor ===
all_SignP <- lapply(factors, function(f) {
  res_GSEA_tb <- read.delim(file.path(input_dir, paste0("GSEA_GOBP_Factor", f, ".txt")))
  res_GSEA_tb$ID[1:min(10, nrow(res_GSEA_tb))]
})
all_SignP_uq <- unique(unlist(all_SignP))
# === Filter to retain only pathways with significant FDR in at least one factor ===
signif_pathways <- unique(unlist(lapply(factors, function(f) {
  res <- read.delim(file.path(input_dir, paste0("GSEA_GOBP_Factor", f, ".txt")))
  sig <- res[res$p.adjust < 0.05, "ID"]
  return(sig)
})))

# Keep only those pathways that are both in the top 10 and significant somewhere
all_SignP_uq <- intersect(all_SignP_uq, signif_pathways)

# === Collect enrichment results for selected pathways ===
comp_tables <- lapply(factors, function(f) {
  res_GSEA_tb <- read.delim(file.path(input_dir, paste0("GSEA_GOBP_Factor", f, ".txt")))
  # rownames(res_GSEA_tb) <- res_GSEA_tb$Description
  rownames(res_GSEA_tb) <- res_GSEA_tb$ID
  res_GSEA_sel <- res_GSEA_tb[intersect(all_SignP_uq, rownames(res_GSEA_tb)), ]
  return(res_GSEA_sel)
})
names(comp_tables) <- paste0("Factor", factors)

# === Prepare NES table ===
summary_tb_NES <- do.call("cbind", lapply(1:length(comp_tables), function(i) {
  tb <- comp_tables[[i]]
  res_df <- data.frame(NES = tb$NES, row.names = gsub("GOBP_", "", rownames(tb)))
  colnames(res_df) <- names(comp_tables)[i]
  return(res_df)
}))
hr_rows <- hclust(dist(summary_tb_NES))
hr_cols <- hclust(dist(t(summary_tb_NES)))

# === Prepare FDR table and stars ===
summary_tb_padj <- do.call("cbind", lapply(1:length(comp_tables), function(i) {
  tb <- comp_tables[[i]]
  res_df <- data.frame(padj = tb$p.adjust, row.names = gsub("GOBP_", "", rownames(tb)))
  colnames(res_df) <- names(comp_tables)[i]
  return(res_df)
}))
pval_to_stars <- function(p) {
  sapply(p, function(x) {
    if (is.na(x)) return("")
    else if (x < 0.001) return("***")
    else if (x < 0.01) return("**")
    else if (x < 0.05) return("*")
    else return("")
  })
}

# Apply across summary_tb_padj to create cellnotes
cellnotes <- apply(summary_tb_padj, 2, pval_to_stars)
# === Plot heatmap ===
library(gplots)
library(RColorBrewer)

Reds_palette <- brewer.pal(n = 7, name = "Reds")
myPalette <- c("lightgray", Reds_palette[c(1,3,5,7)])
colnames(summary_tb_NES) <- c(1,2,3,4,5,6)

cell_fun = function(j, i, x, y, width, height, fill) {
  grid.text(cellnotes[i, j], x, y, gp = gpar(fontsize = 6, col = "black"))
  
  # Get corresponding padj value
  pval <- summary_tb_padj[i, j]
  
  # Draw black box if significant
  if (!is.na(pval) && pval < 0.05) {
    grid.rect(x, y, width, height, gp = gpar(col = "black", fill = NA, lwd = 1))
  }
}

ht <- Heatmap(
  as.matrix(summary_tb_NES),
  heatmap_legend_param = list(),
  name = "NES",
  column_title = "Factor",
  column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 13.2, fontface = "bold"),
  col = colorRamp2(
    c(min(summary_tb_NES, na.rm=TRUE), 0, max(summary_tb_NES, na.rm=TRUE)), 
    c("blue", "white", "red")
  ),
  cell_fun = function(j, i, x, y, width, height, fill) {
    grid.text(cellnotes[i, j], x, y, gp = gpar(fontsize = 6, col = "black"))
    pval <- summary_tb_padj[i, j]
    if (!is.na(pval) && pval < 0.05) {
      grid.rect(x, y, width, height, gp = gpar(col = "black", fill = NA, lwd = 1))
    }
  },
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_columns = FALSE,
  width = unit(4, "cm"),
  row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10),
  column_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold", just = "left"),
  column_names_side = "top",
  column_names_rot = 0
)
pdf(paste0(out_path,"/S6A_GSEA_GOBP_C5_heatmap.pdf"), height = 14, width = 10)
draw(ht, heatmap_legend_side = "left", padding = unit(c(1, 1,1, 11), "cm"))
dev.off()
```

# S6B supp: Association LF5 and LCNEC
```{r}
library(ggpubr)
factors <- get_factors(model, as.data.frame = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::select(- group) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = "factor", values_from = value) %>%
  column_to_rownames("sample")

clin <- samples_metadata(model) %>%
  dplyr::select(-group)

factors <- factors[clin$sample,]

temp <- factors %>%
  rownames_to_column("Immucan_id") %>%
  left_join(clin %>%
              rownames_to_column("Immucan_id"), by = "Immucan_id")

ggplot(temp %>%
         mutate(is_LCNEC = simple_histology == "LCNEC"), aes(x = is_LCNEC, y = Factor4)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  stat_compare_means(method = "t.test")  # adds p-value from t-test

ggplot(temp, aes(x = simple_histology, y = Factor4)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  stat_compare_means(method = "t.test")

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggpubr)

highlight_groups <- c("LCNEC", "Other")

temp <- temp %>%
  mutate(
    hist_group = ifelse(simple_histology %in% highlight_groups, "Highlighted", "Other"),
    shape_group = case_when(
      simple_histology == "LCNEC" ~ "LCNEC",
      simple_histology == "Other" ~ "Other",
      TRUE ~ "LUSC/LUAD/Adenosquamous/Unknown"
    ),
    shape_group = factor(shape_group, levels = c("LCNEC", "Other", "LUSC/LUAD/Adenosquamous/Unknown"))
  )

# Define shapes: triangle (17), square (15), circle (16)
shape_values <- c("LCNEC" = 8, "Other" = 0, "LUSC/LUAD/Adenosquamous/Unknown" = 16)

gg <- ggplot(temp, aes(x = hist_group, y = Factor4, fill = hist_group)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_jitter(aes(shape = shape_group), width = 0.2, alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = shape_values, name = "Histology") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Highlighted" = "firebrick", "Other" = "grey70"), guide = "none") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Highlighted" = "LCNEC + Other", "Other" = "Rest")) +  # relabel x-axis
  stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test",label.y = 4.5,size = 5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14),
    
    #legend.position = "bottom"
  ) +
  labs(y = "Factor4")+
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-4,5))
gg

ggsave(paste0(out_path,"/S6B_Factor5_LCNEC_OTHER.pdf"), plot = gg,device = "pdf",width = 7,height = 4.5)
```

# S6C supp: Factor1 Survival analysis

## overall
```{r}
## survival analysis
library(survminer)
library(survivalAnalysis)
library(survival)

factors <- get_factors(model, as.data.frame = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::select(- group) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = "factor", values_from = value) %>%
  column_to_rownames("sample")

clin <- samples_metadata(model)
cur_dat <- factors %>%
  rownames_to_column("sample") %>%
  full_join(clin, by = "sample")

p.list <- list()
for (i in names(factors)) {
    surv_ob <- Surv(cur_dat$time_to_event,cur_dat$censoring)
    p.list[[i]] <- coxph( as.formula(paste0("surv_ob ~ ",i)),
                          data = cur_dat)
  # }
}

df <- data.frame(HR = unlist(lapply(p.list, function(x){summary(x)$coefficients[,"exp(coef)"]})),
                 p.value = unlist(lapply(p.list, function(x){summary(x)$coefficients[, "Pr(>|z|)"]})),
                 lower_conf = unlist(lapply(p.list, function(x){summary(x)$conf.int[, "lower .95"]})),
                 upper_conf = unlist(lapply(p.list, function(x){summary(x)$conf.int[, "upper .95"]}))) 


custom_order <- c("Factor10", "Factor9", "Factor8", "Factor7", "Factor6", "Factor5", "Factor4", "Factor3", "Factor2", "Factor1")


library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1.  Pull out the Factor 1 row from each data-frame
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
plot_df <- df %>% 
  select( HR, lower_conf, upper_conf, p.value) %>%
  mutate(Cohort = rownames(df))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2.  Forest plot on a log scale
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
p1 <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(y = Cohort, x = HR, col = Cohort)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower_conf, xmax = upper_conf), height = 0.25) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(
    x = "HR",
    y = NULL,
    title = "Factor 1",
    subtitle = sprintf(
      "LUAD p = %.3g   |   LUSC p = %.3g",
      plot_df$p.value[plot_df$Cohort == "LUAD"],
      plot_df$p.value[plot_df$Cohort == "LUSC"]
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("LUAD" = "blue", "LUSC" = "orange"))

print(plot_df)

p1
ggsave(paste0(out_path,"/S6C_Factor1_LUAD_LUSC.pdf"), plot = p1, device = "pdf", width = 4, height = 3)
```
## LUAD/LUSC
```{r}
## survival analysis
library(survminer)
library(survivalAnalysis)
library(survival)

factors <- get_factors(model, as.data.frame = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::select(- group) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = "factor", values_from = value) %>%
  column_to_rownames("sample")

clin <- samples_metadata(model)
cur_dat <- factors %>%
  rownames_to_column("sample") %>%
  full_join(clin, by = "sample") %>%
  filter(simple_histology == "Adenocarcinoma")

p.list <- list()
for (i in names(factors)) {
    surv_ob <- Surv(cur_dat$time_to_event,cur_dat$censoring)
    p.list[[i]] <- coxph( as.formula(paste0("surv_ob ~ ",i)),
                          data = cur_dat)
  # }
}

df_luad <- data.frame(HR = unlist(lapply(p.list, function(x){summary(x)$coefficients[,"exp(coef)"]})),
                 p.value = unlist(lapply(p.list, function(x){summary(x)$coefficients[, "Pr(>|z|)"]})),
                 lower_conf = unlist(lapply(p.list, function(x){summary(x)$conf.int[, "lower .95"]})),
                 upper_conf = unlist(lapply(p.list, function(x){summary(x)$conf.int[, "upper .95"]}))) 


custom_order <- c("Factor10", "Factor9", "Factor8", "Factor7", "Factor6", "Factor5", "Factor4", "Factor3", "Factor2", "Factor1")

# LUSC

library(survminer)
library(survivalAnalysis)
library(survival)

factors <- get_factors(model, as.data.frame = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::select(- group) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = "factor", values_from = value) %>%
  column_to_rownames("sample")

clin <- samples_metadata(model)
cur_dat <- factors %>%
  rownames_to_column("sample") %>%
  full_join(clin, by = "sample") %>%
  filter(simple_histology == "Squamous cell carcinoma")

p.list <- list()
for (i in names(factors)) {
    surv_ob <- Surv(cur_dat$time_to_event,cur_dat$censoring)
    p.list[[i]] <- coxph( as.formula(paste0("surv_ob ~ ",i)),
                          data = cur_dat)
  # }
}

df_lusc <- data.frame(HR = unlist(lapply(p.list, function(x){summary(x)$coefficients[,"exp(coef)"]})),
                 p.value = unlist(lapply(p.list, function(x){summary(x)$coefficients[, "Pr(>|z|)"]})),
                 lower_conf = unlist(lapply(p.list, function(x){summary(x)$conf.int[, "lower .95"]})),
                 upper_conf = unlist(lapply(p.list, function(x){summary(x)$conf.int[, "upper .95"]}))) 

custom_order <- c("Factor10", "Factor9", "Factor8", "Factor7", "Factor6", "Factor5", "Factor4", "Factor3", "Factor2", "Factor1")


library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1.  Pull out the Factor 1 row from each data-frame
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
plot_df <- bind_rows(
  df_luad %>%                        # LUAD results
    dplyr::slice(match("Factor1", rownames(.))) %>% 
    mutate(Cohort = "LUAD"),
  df_lusc %>%                        # LUSC results
    dplyr::slice(match("Factor1", rownames(.))) %>% 
    mutate(Cohort = "LUSC")
) %>% 
  select(Cohort, HR, lower_conf, upper_conf, p.value)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2.  Forest plot on a log scale
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
p1 <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(y = Cohort, x = HR, col = Cohort)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower_conf, xmax = upper_conf), height = 0.25) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(
    x = "HR",
    y = NULL,
    title = "Factor 1",
    subtitle = sprintf(
      "LUAD p = %.3g   |   LUSC p = %.3g",
      plot_df$p.value[plot_df$Cohort == "LUAD"],
      plot_df$p.value[plot_df$Cohort == "LUSC"]
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("LUAD" = "blue", "LUSC" = "orange"))

print(plot_df)

p1
ggsave(paste0(out_path,"/S6C_Factor1_LUAD_LUSC.pdf"), plot = p1, device = "pdf", width = 4, height = 3)
```
